summary: Tested Smart Preprocessing Recommendations feature in PreprocessView.js. All features working correctly: Smart Recommendations card displays with severity badges (HIGH/MED/LOW), Apply All button applies all recommendations, individual Apply buttons work, all 4 preprocessing sections (Missing Values, Feature Scaling, Outlier Treatment, Feature Selection) are present with proper data-testid attributes. No React Hook dependency warnings found in console - the useMemo/useCallback ordering fix is working correctly.

critical_code_review_comments: PreprocessView.js hooks are properly ordered: useMemo for recommendations at line 55 comes after all useState/useCallback declarations
The 'update' function is wrapped in useCallback with [setPreprocessConfig] dependency - no missing dependency warning
applyRecommendation useCallback correctly includes [setPreprocessConfig, update] dependencies
applyAllRecommendations useCallback correctly includes [recommendations, applyRecommendation] dependencies
Early return guard at line 163 comes AFTER all hooks - React rules of hooks satisfied
seed_data_creation: None - used existing Loan Approval sample dataset
context_for_next_testing_agent: Smart Preprocessing Recommendations feature is fully working. The PreprocessView.js had React Hook ordering issues that were fixed - all hooks (useMemo, useCallback) are now declared before any early returns. The recommendations engine analyzes dataProfile and produces recommendations for: (1) missing values, (2) outliers via IQR, (3) feature scale variance, (4) high-cardinality categoricals, (5) low-variance features. Each recommendation has severity (high/medium/low) and an Apply button. Apply All applies all recommendations at once. The Loan Approval dataset triggers 2 recommendations: outliers (HIGH) and scaling (MED). No missing values recommendation since dataset is complete.
